post.description:
Create a new organization in the platform. The authenticated user automatically becomes the owner.
    
**Required Permission:** `platform:organization:create` or `org:organization:create`

**Example Request:**
```json
{
  "name": "Acme Corporation",
  "slug": "acme-corp",
  "tier": "enterprise",
  "description": "A leading technology company",
  "isBusiness": true,
  "registrationNo": "CPR/2010/245678",
  "countryOfRegistration": "KE",
  "industry": "manufacturing",
  "type": "ltd"
}
```

200.description: Organization created successfully with tier, owner, and default settings initialized

example.message: Insufficient permissions to perform this action

post.description:
Send an invitation to a user by email to join your organization with a specific role.
    
The recipient will receive an email with a secure invite link that expires in 7 days (configurable).

**Roles:**
- `owner` - Full control over the organization
- `admin` - Manage members, settings, and resources
- `member` - Standard access to resources
- `viewer` - Read-only access

**Example Request:**
```json
{
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "role": "member",
  "expiryDays": 7
}
```

Verify a user's email address using the token sent via email.
    
    This endpoint:
    - Validates the verification token against the user's email
    - Marks the email as verified if token is valid and not expired
    - Activates the user account after successful verification
    - Tokens expire after 24 hours
    
    **Security:**
    - Token comparison is case-sensitive
    - Failed verification attempts are logged
    - Returns generic error message for security (doesn't reveal if email exists)

Resend the email verification email to a user who hasn't verified yet.
    
    This endpoint:
    - Generates a new verification token (invalidates the old one)
    - Sends a new verification email with the token
    - Only works for users who haven't verified their email yet
    - Uses app-specific branding based on X-Client-App header
    
    **Rate Limiting:**
    - Consider implementing rate limiting to prevent abuse
    - All resend attempts are logged for monitoring
    
    **Security:**
    - Returns success message even if email doesn't exist (prevents email enumeration)
    - New token invalidates previous tokens

Enable multi-factor authentication for the current user.

**Supported Methods:**
- `totp`: Time-based One-Time Password (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)
- `sms`: SMS-based verification (requires phone number)
- `email`: Email-based verification (requires email)

**Example Request (TOTP):**
```json
{
  "method": "totp"
}
```

**Example Request (SMS):**
```json
{
  "method": "sms",
  "phoneNumber": "+1234567890"
}
```

**Response includes:**
- `secret`: TOTP secret (for totp method)
- `qrCode`: QR code as base64 data URL for totp method
- `backupCodes`: Array of 10 backup codes (save these securely!)

200.description: MFA enabled successfully. Response includes TOTP secret/QR code (for TOTP) and backup codes to save securely.

409.description: MFA already enabled for this user

post.description:
Verify MFA token to complete authentication or validate MFA setup.

**For TOTP:**
Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

**For SMS/Email:**
Enter the code received via SMS or email.

**Using Backup Codes:**
Set `isBackupCode: true` and provide one of your backup codes.

**Example Request:**
```json
{
  "token": "123456"
}
```

**Example with Backup Code:**
```json
{
  "token": "6F65BF6B",
  "isBackupCode": true
}
```

**Note:** Each backup code can only be used once.

delete.description:
Disable multi-factor authentication for the current user.

**Warning:** This will:
- Disable MFA protection for the account
- Invalidate all backup codes
- Clear TOTP secret

**Security Recommendation:**
**Security Note:**
Consider requiring password confirmation or current MFA verification before allowing disable operation in production environments for enhanced security.

get.description:
Get current MFA configuration status for the authenticated user.

**Response when MFA is enabled:**
Returns MFA configuration with status, method, and verification state.

**Response when MFA is not configured:**
Returns `null` in the data field.

**Use this endpoint to:**
- Check if user has MFA enabled
- Display MFA status in user settings
- Determine which MFA method is active

post.description:
Create a new account using email and password.
    
This endpoint:
    - Validates email format and password strength
    - Checks for existing users with the same email
    - Hashes the password using bcrypt
    - Creates an activated user account
    - Returns JWT access and refresh tokens
    
    **Password Requirements:**
    - Minimum 8 characters
    - At least one uppercase letter
    - At least one lowercase letter
    - At least one number
    - At least one special character

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

409.description:
Email already registered

Conflict - Resource already exists or state conflict

post.description:
Initiate phone number registration by sending an OTP.
    
    This endpoint:
    - Validates phone number format (E.164)
    - Checks for existing users with the same phone
    - Generates a 6-digit OTP
    - Sends OTP via SMS (using Twilio)
    - Returns identifier for OTP verification
    
    **Note**: Trial Twilio accounts can only send SMS to verified numbers.
    
    **Next Step**: Call POST /auth/register/otp/verify with the OTP code.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

409.description:
Phone number already registered

Conflict - Resource already exists or state conflict

500.description:
SMS sending failed (check Twilio configuration)

Internal Server Error

post.description:
Create account with identity verification details and password.
    
    This endpoint:
    - Creates user account with email and password
    - Creates participant profile with ID information
    - Initiates KYC verification request for identity check
    - Sends email verification
    - Returns verification instructions
    
    **Required Fields:**
    - firstName: User's first name
    - identificationType: Type of ID (national_id, passport, drivers_license)
    - identificationNo: ID document number
    - email: User's email address
    - phone: Phone number in E.164 format
    - password: Secure password (min 8 chars, mixed case, numbers, special chars)
    
    **Next Steps:**
    1. Verify email via link sent to provided address
    2. KYC verification will be processed automatically
    3. Login after email verification

get.description:
Retrieve information about an invitation using its token.
    
This is a public endpoint used to display invite details before the user accepts it.

**Example Response:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "organizationId": "uuid",
  "organizationName": "Acme Corp",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "role": "member",
  "status": "pending",
  "invitedAt": "2026-05-08T10:00:00Z",
  "expiresAt": "2026-05-15T10:00:00Z",
  "isExpired": false,
  "isValid": true
}
```

post.description:
Accept an invitation to join an organization.
    
**Two scenarios:**

1. **New User** - Creating an account via invitation:
   - Provide `token`, `firstName`, `lastName`, and `password`
   - A new user account is created automatically
   - User is added to the organization with the invited role
   - Returns authentication tokens to log the user in

2. **Existing User** - Accepting while logged in:
   - Provide `token` only
   - Must be logged in (JWT in Authorization header)
   - User is added to the organization
   - Returns updated organization membership info

**New User Example:**
```json
{
  "token": "secure-invite-token-here",
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "password": "SecureP@ssw0rd"
}
```

**Existing User Example:**
```json
{
  "token": "secure-invite-token-here"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "userId": "uuid",
  "organizationId": "uuid",
  "role": "member",
  "isNewUser": true,
  "accessToken": "jwt-token",
  "refreshToken": "refresh-token"
}
```

409.description: Already a member of this organization

post.description:
Initiates a new voice call to a participant or person of interest.
      
      **Features:**
      - Supports both Participants and Persons of Interest
      - Automated calls via VAPI integration
      - Live or recorded call types
      - Optional approval workflow for high-cost calls
      - Automatic usage tracking and token consumption
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Must provide either participantId OR personOfInterestId
      - Phone number must be in E.164 format (+1234567890)
      - User must have VOICE_CALL CREATE permission
      - Organization must have sufficient token balance
      
      **Approval Workflow:**
      Calls may require approval based on:
      - Estimated cost exceeds threshold
      - Target is high-risk person of interest
      - Call initiated outside business hours
      - International destination

example.message: Phone number must be in E.164 format (+1234567890)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing JWT token

post.description:
Manually ends an active voice call.
      
      **Actions Performed:**
      - Terminates call on VAPI provider
      - Records end timestamp
      - Calculates final duration
      - Updates call status to 'completed'
      - Triggers voice analysis workflow
      - Tracks token usage and billing
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must be in 'ringing' or 'in_progress' status
      - User must be call initiator OR have VOICE_CALL UPDATE permission

get.description:
Retrieves complete call details including:
      - Call metadata and status
      - Recording URL (if available)
      - Transcript (if available)
      - Analysis results (if completed)
      - Event timeline
      - Usage and billing information
      
      **Requirements:**
      - User must be call initiator OR have VOICE_CALL READ permission

Retrieves paginated call history for the organization.
      
      **Features:**
      - Pagination support
      - Multiple filter options
      - Sorted by most recent
      - Row-level security (users see only their calls unless admin)
      
      **Filters:**
      - participantId: Filter by participant
      - personOfInterestId: Filter by person of interest
      - status: Filter by call status
      - dateFrom: Start date for date range
      - dateTo: End date for date range
      - page: Page number (default: 1)
      - limit: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

Retrieves all currently active calls (ringing or in-progress) for the organization.
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Real-time call monitoring dashboard
      - Call center supervision
      - Live metrics tracking
      
      **Returns:**
      Calls with status 'ringing' or 'in_progress', sorted by start time.
      Includes monitoring URLs if available for real-time control.

Retrieves comprehensive call statistics for the organization.
      
      **Metrics Included:**
      - Total calls (all time and period-specific)
      - Calls by status (completed, failed, in-progress)
      - Average call duration
      - Total call duration
      - Total cost
      - Success rate
      - Calls by date range
      
      **Filters:**
      - dateFrom: Start date for statistics
      - dateTo: End date for statistics

Retrieves call details with fresh data from VAPI API.
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Get real-time call status during active calls
      - Verify call completion and get final costs
      - Access latest recording and transcript URLs
      - Debugging and support
      
      **Returns:**
      - Local database call record
      - Fresh VAPI API response data
      - Merged view with latest information
      
      **Note:** Only works for calls with valid external VAPI call IDs.

200.description: Call details with VAPI data retrieved successfully

404.description: Call not found or no external VAPI ID

get.summary: Get call with fresh VAPI data

get.description:
Retrieves all calls associated with a specific participant.
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - View participant call history
      - Track verification attempts
      - Audit participant interactions
      
      **Pagination:**
      - page: Page number (default: 1)
      - limit: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

Retrieves all calls associated with a specific person of interest.
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Fraud investigation call history
      - Track POI contact attempts
      - Review investigation interactions

200.description: Person of interest calls retrieved successfully

get.summary: List calls by person of interest

get.description:
Retrieves the recording URL for a call.
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must be completed
      - Recording must be available
      
      **Returns:**
      - Recording URL (direct link to audio file)
      - Recording metadata (format, duration, size if available)

404.description: Call not found or recording not available

get.description:
Retrieves the transcript for a call.
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must be completed
      - Transcript must be available
      
      **Returns:**
      - Full transcript text
      - Segmented transcript with timestamps (if available)
      - Speaker identification

example.transcriptText: Hello, this is a verification call...

segments.speaker: agent
segments.text: Hello, this is a verification call.

404.description: Call not found or transcript not available

post.description:
Analyzes the call recording using InsightGenie AI for fraud detection and stress analysis.
      
      **Process:**
      1. Retrieves recording URL from call
      2. Sends audio to InsightGenie for analysis
      3. Returns comprehensive voice analysis results including:
         - Fraud probability score
         - Stress level indicators
         - Deception indicators
         - Voice metrics (pitch, energy, speech rate)
         - Risk flags and recommendations
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must have a recording available
      - User must have VOICE_CALL UPDATE permission
      
      **Optional:**
      - Set forceReanalysis=true to re-analyze already analyzed recordings

parameters.name: forceReanalysis
parameters.description: Force re-analysis even if already analyzed

results.summary: Low risk. Voice patterns appear normal with minimal stress indicators.

404.description: Call not found or recording not available

post.description:
Manually fetches the latest analysis results from InsightGenie for a call.
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Check if async analysis has completed
      - Manually refresh results instead of waiting for polling
      - Debugging analysis status issues
      
      **Process:**
      1. Checks if call has pending/in-progress InsightGenie analysis
      2. Fetches latest results from InsightGenie API
      3. Updates call record with results if complete
      4. Returns current analysis status
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must have an active InsightGenie analysis ID
      - User must have VOICE_CALL UPDATE permission

404.description: Call not found or no InsightGenie analysis found

get.description:
Retrieves real-time monitoring URLs for an active call from VAPI.
      
      **Monitor URLs:**
      - listenUrl: WebSocket URL for real-time audio streaming
      - controlUrl: HTTP URL for call control operations (mute, transfer, etc.)
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Call must be active (ringing or in-progress)
      - User must have VOICE_CALL READ permission
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Real-time call monitoring dashboard
      - Live call supervision
      - Call quality monitoring

404.description: Call not found or monitor URLs not available

get.description:
Retrieves underlying transport provider details (Twilio) for a call.
      
      **Transport Details:**
      - provider: Transport provider (e.g., "twilio")
      - callSid: Twilio Call SID for debugging
      - accountSid: Twilio Account SID
      - timestamp: When transport was established
      
      **Requirements:**
      - User must have VOICE_CALL READ permission
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Troubleshooting call issues
      - Integration with Twilio debugging tools
      - Call quality investigation

404.description: Call not found or transport details not available

get.description:
Retrieves all active calls with fresh VAPI data merged for each call.
      
      **Enhanced Data Includes:**
      - Database call record
      - Fresh VAPI status, cost, duration
      - Monitor URLs (listenUrl, controlUrl) for active calls
      - Transport details (Twilio callSid)
      - Recording and transcript status
      
      **Requirements:**
      - User must have VOICE_CALL READ permission
      - Returns calls from user's active organization
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Real-time call monitoring dashboard
      - Live call supervision with control URLs
      - Active call quality monitoring

200.description: Active calls with VAPI details retrieved successfully

get.summary: Get active calls with VAPI details

post.description:
Retrieves fresh VAPI data for multiple calls in a single request.
      
      **Process:**
      1. Accepts array of call IDs
      2. Fetches each call from database
      3. Retrieves fresh VAPI data for each
      4. Returns merged data for all calls
      
      **Requirements:**
      - User must have VOICE_CALL READ permission
      - All calls must belong to user's organization
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Bulk status refresh for dashboard
      - Batch cost updates
      - Multi-call monitoring

callIds.description: Array of call IDs to fetch VAPI data for

post.summary: Bulk get VAPI status for multiple calls

post.description:
Registers a participant's voice with InsightGenie for future verification.
      
      **Process:**
      1. Upload audio sample (min 45 seconds recommended)
      2. Store voice profile with participant ID
      3. Enable future voice verification against stored reference
      
      **Requirements:**
      - Audio file must be clear with minimal background noise
      - Minimum 45 seconds of audio recommended
      - User must have VOICE_CALL CREATE permission
      
      **Use Cases:**
      - Initial participant enrollment
      - Voice authentication setup
      - Fraud prevention through speaker verification

post.description:
Finalize the onboarding process after all steps are completed. This endpoint validates all requirements are met before completing onboarding. It will:
- Verify all onboarding steps are completed
- Mark user.hasCompletedOnboarding = true
- Activate user account (user.status = active)
- Mark organization.isSetupComplete = true

**Important:** 
- This endpoint requires X-Organization-ID header (NOT in request body)
- Request body should only contain userId
- Call this only after completing all onboarding steps via /complete-step.

parameters.name: X-Organization-ID
parameters.description: Organization ID to mark as setup complete (REQUIRED). This is the organization being onboarded.

info.title: Verify Group Platform API
info.description:
Enterprise multi-tenant platform with DDD/CQRS architecture

## Authentication
All endpoints (except public ones) require JWT authentication.
Include the JWT token in the Authorization header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>
```

## Multi-Tenancy
For organization-scoped requests, include the organization ID in the header:
```
X-Organization-ID: <organization-uuid>
```

For team or department context:
```
X-Team-ID: <team-uuid>
X-Department-ID: <department-uuid>
```

## Response Format
All successful responses follow this structure:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "statusCode": 200,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": { ... },
  "metadata": {
    "requestId": "uuid-v4",
    "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
    "duration": 45
  }
}
```

## Error Format
All error responses follow this structure:
```json
{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "message": "Error description",
  "error": "ErrorType",
  "code": "ERROR_CODE",
  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
  "path": "/api/v1/endpoint",
  "correlationId": "uuid-v4",
  "metadata": { ... }
}
```

## Permissions & RBAC
Each endpoint documents required permissions. The format is:
`scope:resource:action`

Examples:
- `platform:user:create` - Platform-level user creation
- `org:kyc:approve` - Organization-level KYC approval
- `team:report:read` - Team-level report reading

## Rate Limiting
API requests are rate-limited based on your subscription tier.
Rate limit headers are included in responses:
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Requests per window
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Remaining requests
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Window reset time

isBusiness.description: Whether this organization is business or individual

countryOfRegistration.description: ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code of registration

password.description: User password (min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number, special char)

Phone number in E.164 format (required for SMS OTP)

context.description: Authentication context to resolve account source. Defaults to 'client' when omitted.

token.description: Password reset token received via email

newPassword.description: New password (min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number, special char)
